title: Enterprise Schema Evolution Benchmark
subtitle: MongoDB Atlas Local 8.3.4 vs Postgres 16.14 — 40-entity schema, live database, real AI agents

task.description: Add a preferredPaymentMethod field to the accounts entity and update the order summary query to include it. The agent must read and understand the existing 40-entity schema before safely making the change.
task.why: This is the enterprise day-to-day: a developer gets a ticket to add a field and must understand the existing schema, find where the field goes, update the code, and not break anything.

execution.tokenSource: Real CLI usage events (deduplicated by message ID)

caveat: Within-agent comparison only. Each agent is measured against itself across the three database designs; one agent's absolute numbers are never compared to the other's, because the two CLIs count tokens incompatibly.
proofOfNoBias: Both agents tested — different models, different companies, same conclusion
Three lanes tested — MongoDB, Postgres-normalized, Postgres-JSONB (dose-response)
Pre-registered hypotheses — H1 and H2 stated before runs, not after
Vanilla mode — no skills, no code reviews, no subagents, no workflow overhead
Live database — tests query the real DB directly, not mocks
Anti-cheat — source-pattern scan, protected files, cheat-signal detection
5 repeats per cell — medians and IQR, not single-run anecdotes
Within-agent comparison — each agent compared only to itself, never cross-CLI
Simple tasks showed Postgres winning — the benchmark reported that honestly
All evidence committed — raw transcripts, diffs, test logs, token counts
